title: Machine First Planning Chain
goal: Replace prose-first authored execplans with genuinely machine-first canonical planning state and derived human-readable views.
Move structured references and proportional review residue into durable, queryable planning records.
non_goals: Do not create a hidden second planning store.
Do not close the full lane based on a single proving slice.
Do not force every planning surface into heavy structure when compact prose still suffices.

intent.outcome: Continue the reopened machine-first lane beyond the first canonical sidecar proof by extracting remaining Markdown-authored planning truth, structured references, and proportional review residue into canonical records.
intent.constraints: Canonical machine-first state stays checked in beside human-readable views, remains git-reviewable, and does not replace compact planning outputs with heavier defaults.
intent.latitude: Use a real repo slice as the proving path for the next extraction step and choose the smallest remaining Markdown-authored truth that still creates restart or review cost.
intent.escalation: Escalate if the work would require a hidden planning database, break compact planning surfaces, or collapse bounded review residue into broad archival prose.

execution.next_step: Pick the next real planning slice that still depends on Markdown-authored truth, then extract structured references and proportional review residue through the canonical sidecar path.
execution.proof: A real active planning slice uses canonical machine-first records for structured references and bounded review residue, with planning summary/report/check surfaces all staying honest and compact.

invariants: Canonical state stays checked in beside the human view.
Structured references are explicit and typed enough to be queryable.
Review residue stays proportional and does not turn every execplan into a long notebook.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: replace prose-first authored execplans with machine-first canonical planning state, explicit structured references, and proportional review residue with derived human-readable views

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: planned: the reopened lane can move from the first sidecar proof to the unfinished typed-reference and review-residue follow-through instead of leaving the lane in contradictory archive state.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: broad workflow-substrate redesign, unrelated startup discoverability fixes, and local-only integration boundaries.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: premature closeout showed that honest continuation and structured residue are not optional polish; they are central to keeping weaker-agent planning from drifting.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: none yet; this is a prepared plan.
iterative_follow_through.validation still needed: prove that structured references and proportional review residue are canonical enough to reduce restart and review cost on a real slice.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: use one active slice to canonicalize structured references and bounded review residue against the current sidecar path.

intent_interpretation.literal request: prepare a full execplan for the machine-first planning chain lane
intent_interpretation.inferred intended outcome: restore the lane as a real continuing plan, shaped around the unfinished follow-through after the first sidecar slice rather than pretending the machine-first migration is complete
intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: plan the lane around the next real extraction step: structured references and proportional review residue on top of the already-shipped canonical sidecar path
intent_interpretation.review guidance: reject work that only adds more canonical files without reducing remaining Markdown-authored planning truth or without keeping review residue proportional

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "planning_summary or execplan or review"; uv run pytest tests/test_contract_tooling.py -q; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run python scripts/check/check_contract_tooling_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json --profile full
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: stop before introducing new planning storage backends, replacing compact planning outputs, or rewriting archive flows beyond the bounded canonicalization needed for the active slice
execution_bounds.stop before touching: workspace runtime posture surfaces, AGENTS.md startup routing, or local-only integration roots

stop_conditions.stop when: the next extraction step cannot be expressed as a bounded canonicalization of one remaining Markdown-authored truth surface
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: the work would require redesigning the whole planning package rather than finishing the next machine-first follow-through slice
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: the effort starts becoming broad planning cleanup instead of structured references and proportional review residue
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: summary/report/check surfaces become less compact or less honest after the new canonical data lands

context_budget.live working set: current sidecar contract, reopened lane evidence, typed references expectations, review residue contract, planning package tests and checks
context_budget.recoverable later: older archive history and broader packaging context unless a specific regression appears
context_budget.externalize before shift: which planning truths still live mainly in Markdown, the exact next canonicalization target, and the proof that compact surfaces remain adequate afterward
context_budget.tiny resumability note: do not redo the first sidecar proof; target the unfinished typed-reference and review-residue follow-through on one real slice
context_budget.context-shift triggers: after picking the next canonicalization target, after changing schema/checker expectations, or before archive/closeout decisions

delegated_judgment.requested outcome: finish the machine-first follow-through that remained after the first sidecar slice
delegated_judgment.hard constraints: no hidden store; compact outputs stay compact; review residue remains proportional; lane closure requires explicit larger-intent proof
delegated_judgment.agent may decide locally: which remaining Markdown-authored truth to extract first, exact typed-reference shape, and the narrowest review-residue schema that still reduces ambiguity
delegated_judgment.escalate when: the worker would need to broaden into storage redesign, replace human-readable views, or claim the full lane is complete without follow-through proof

references.label: closeout trust evidence
references.locator: finding on delegated handoff and likely premature closeout signals

active_milestone.scope: Use one real active planning slice to canonicalize structured references and bounded review residue on top of the already-shipped sidecar path.
active_milestone.optional_deps: gh issue refresh before implementation if #261/#280/#282 moved materially

immediate_next_action: Archive this completed machine-first lane cleanly and hand activation to the next roadmap lane.
invariants: Canonical planning state stays checked in.
Human-readable planning views remain derived and reviewable.
Bounded slice completion and lane completion remain explicitly distinct.
contract_decisions_to_freeze: Structured references should be typed and queryable from canonical plan records.
Review residue should be proportional and purpose-shaped, not open-ended journals.
A slice archive cannot silently imply lane completion when required follow-on remains.
open_questions_to_close: Which review-artifact fields should remain as durable residue after active validation ends?
Should proportional review residue stay in live review records only, or also surface through a tighter compact closeout projection?
What is the smallest retention rule that reduces parallel-archive pressure without weakening review trust?
validation_commands: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "planning_summary or execplan or review"
uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json --profile full
completion_criteria: One real planning slice uses canonical structured references instead of Markdown-authored reference truth.
Bounded review residue becomes machine-first enough to reduce ambiguity without inflating compact planning surfaces.
Planning checks and summaries stay honest and compact after the new canonical data lands.
Lane continuation remains explicit, preventing another premature closeout.

execution_run.what happened: Added a canonical retention block to planning review records, projected that retention data through compact review residue, updated the review contract/template, and proved the real referenced machine-first review artifact now carries explicit closeout shape instead of leaving retention only in finding prose.
execution_run.validations run: uv run pytest packages/planning/tests/test_installer.py -q -k "review or planning_summary"; uv run python scripts/check/check_planning_surfaces.py; uv run agentic-workspace summary --format json --profile full
execution_run.result for continuation: The lane now has canonical execplan sidecars, typed references on both execution and roadmap surfaces, and explicit proportional review retention in canonical review records; only final closeout and next-lane activation remain.
execution_run.next step: Archive the completed machine-first lane and activate the next roadmap lane honestly.

finished_run_review.config compliance: respected checked-in planning, workflow, and issue-state requirements for the bounded slice
finished_run_review.misinterpretation risk: low for the shipped review-residue slice; moderate if later follow-through again leaves review context only in prose

proof_report.validation proof: focused planning package tests, contract checks, and full-profile summary inspection
proof_report.proof achieved now: Machine-first planning now has canonical execplan state, typed references on both execution and higher-level roadmap surfaces, and explicit proportional review-retention policy carried in canonical review records and compact review residue projections.
proof_report.evidence for \"proof achieved\" state: Focused planning tests passed, the live full-profile summary reports roadmap-lane references plus review-residue retention, and the referenced real review artifact now carries an explicit retention block.

intent_satisfaction.original intent: prepare a full execplan that reopens and bounds the machine-first planning chain lane honestly
intent_satisfaction.evidence of intent satisfaction: the lane finished with canonical execplan state, typed references on execution and roadmap surfaces, and explicit proportional review-retention policy carried in canonical review records.

closure_check.why this decision is honest: the active machine-first lane now satisfies its stated outcome: canonical execplan state is authoritative, typed references span execution and roadmap surfaces, and proportional review residue is explicit in canonical review records instead of living only in prose.
closure_check.evidence carried forward: this execplan, the reopened lane in state.toml, the shipped review-residue projection, the lane-level structured references, the explicit review-retention blocks, and the archived first-slice proof
closure_check.reopen trigger: reopen only if later planning work again depends on Markdown-authored or Markdown-only state for durable active or archived truth

execution_summary.outcome delivered: completed the machine-first planning-chain follow-through with canonical execplan state, typed references across execution and roadmap surfaces, and explicit proportional review-retention policy in canonical review records.
execution_summary.validation confirmed: focused planning tests and live summary inspection
execution_summary.follow-on routed to: archive this execplan, remove the completed machine-first lane from active planning state, and activate the next roadmap lane
execution_summary.post-work posterity capture: the active execplan now records the final machine-first proof before archival cleanup.
execution_summary.knowledge promoted (memory/docs/config): planning review schema/template/README plus summary projections for review retention and roadmap-lane references

drift_log: 2026-04-24: Initial full-lane execplan created from the reopened lane and live follow-on issues.
2026-04-24: Landed compact review-residue projection from typed review references and activated the machine-first execplan-backed slice in checked-in planning state.
2026-04-24: Extended the structured reference model into roadmap lanes so higher-level planning state carries canonical typed references too.
2026-04-24: Added explicit canonical review-retention blocks and projected them through compact review residue so the lane no longer depends on prose-only closeout policy.
